data_in.description: QCpool positional table in txt format exported from Sciex Multiquant (v3.0.3 or later)

data_out.description: Quality overview report (table or figure) showing per-compound CV and signal-trend metrics across QCpool injections

domain_knowledge: Coefficient of variation (CV) quantifies technical reproducibility in metabolomics; lower CV indicates better instrumental stability and measurement precision.
QCpool (pooled quality control sample) is a composite reference material injected at regular intervals during LC-MS/MS sequences to monitor instrumental performance and detect drift.
Signal-trend metrics track intensity changes across sequential injections to identify instrumental degradation, detector saturation, or column fouling.
Sciex Multiquant txt export format contains positional data (retention time, compound identifiers, and integrated signal intensity) that can be parsed to extract per-injection measurements.

direct_checks: verify that inputs include a concrete TXT export file from Sciex Multiquant (> v3.0.3) containing QCpool positional table data
verify that expected_outputs artifact exists and is a named file (CSV/TSV/PDF table or PNG/PDF figure)
verify file_exists: the output report file in the specified format
verify that output report contains at least one column or metric labeled 'CV' or 'coefficient of variation'
verify that output report contains at least one column or metric labeled 'signal-trend' or equivalent trend metric across QCpool injections
script_runs: QComics package executes without error on the provided TXT input and produces the report artifact
expert_review: whether CV calculations are mathematically correct (standard deviation / mean × 100 or equivalent definition)
whether signal-trend metric appropriately captures temporal or injection-order variation in QCpool signal intensity
whether the per-compound metrics are aggregated or presented at appropriate granularity for a 'quality overview'
whether the report layout and visualizations align with stated goal of providing 'quick overview of quality' for metabolomics/lipidomics studies

evidence_snippets.text: [intro] What metrics does QComics compute from QCpool injection data to assess metabolomics or lipidomics study quality?: 'The goal of the `QComics` package is to have a quick overview of the quality of a metabolomics or lipidomics study.'

[intro] QComics processes QCpool samples analyzed with Sciex Multiquant (> v3.0.3) software and exported to txt format, measuring pooled samples at regular intervals during sequences to generate quality assessment data.: 'a pooled sample (QCpool) needs to measured in regular intervals during one or more sequences. These QCpool samples need to be analysed with Sciex Multiquant (> v3.0.3) software and exported to `txt`'

cmd: import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; df = pd.read_csv('{inputs.qcpool_txt}', sep='\t'); compounds = df.groupby('Compound')['Signal'].agg(['mean', 'std']); compounds['CV'] = (compounds['std'] / compounds['mean']) * 100; compounds.to_csv('qcpool_quality_overview_{task_id}.csv'); print(compounds)
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Quality overview report (table or figure) showing per-compound CV and signal-trend metrics across QCpool injections
finding: QComics processes QCpool samples analyzed with Sciex Multiquant (> v3.0.3) software and exported to txt format, measuring pooled samples at regular intervals during sequences to generate quality assessment data.
inputs: QCpool positional table in txt format exported from Sciex Multiquant (v3.0.3 or later)
methodology_summary: Load and parse QCpool positional table from Sciex Multiquant txt export, extracting compound identifiers and signal intensities across sequential injections.
Calculate per-compound coefficient of variation (CV) to assess technical reproducibility across QCpool replicates.
Compute signal-trend metrics (drift, stability index, or slope) to detect instrumental performance changes across the injection sequence.
Aggregate CV and trend results by compound into a summary table with quality classification (e.g., acceptable vs. poor).
Visualization: generate table or figure presenting per-compound CV, trend metrics, and overall quality assessment for rapid interpretation.
Validation: output includes per-compound CV values and trend indicators with quality thresholds suitable for literature reporting or protocol standardization.
missing_information: No explicit description of CV and signal-trend metric definitions or computational formulas in discussion section

task_description: Using a QCpool positional table exported from Sciex Multiquant txt format, compute per-compound coefficient of variation (CV) and signal-trend metrics across QCpool injections to generate a quality overview report.

task_inputs.label: QCpool positional table from Sciex Multiquant txt export

task_objective: Compute per-compound coefficient of variation and signal-trend metrics from QCpool txt export data to generate a quality overview report.
title: Reconstruct quality overview summary from parsed QCpool positional data
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Sciex Multiquant (> v3.0.3).
workflow_description: 1. Load the QCpool positional table from Sciex Multiquant txt export. 2. Parse compound identifiers and signal intensities across sequential QCpool injections. 3. Calculate per-compound coefficient of variation (CV) as a measure of technical reproducibility. 4. Assess signal-trend metrics (e.g., slope, drift, or stability index) across the injection sequence to detect instrumental drift or signal degradation. 5. Aggregate CV and trend results into a summary table or visualization highlighting compounds with acceptable vs. poor quality metrics. 6. Generate a quality overview report (table and/or figure) suitable for rapid assessment of study metabolomics/lipidomics data quality.
